source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That the Cloud SQL Database Instance Requires All Incoming Connections To Use SSL

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That the Cloud SQL Database Instance Requires All Incoming Connections To Use SSL
input_row.description: It is recommended to enforce all incoming connections to SQL database instance to use SSL.
input_row.rationale: SQL database connections if successfully trapped (MITM); can reveal sensitive data like credentials, database queries, query outputs etc. For security, it is recommended to always use SSL encryption when connecting to your instance. This recommendation is applicable for Postgresql, MySql generation 1, MySql generation 2 and SQL Server 2017 instances. Impact: After enforcing SSL requirement for connections, existing client will not be able to communicate with Cloud SQL database instance unless they use SSL encrypted connections to communicate to Cloud SQL database instance.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Click on an instance name to see its configuration overview. 3. In the left-side panel, select Connections. 4. In the Security section, ensure that Allow only SSL connections option is selected. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Get the detailed configuration for every SQL database instance using the following command: gcloud sql instances list --format=json Ensure that section settings: ipConfiguration has the parameter sslMode set to ENCRYPTED_ONLY .
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Click on an instance name to see its configuration overview. 3. In the left-side panel, select Connections. 4. In the security section, select SSL mode as Allow only SSL connections. 5. Under Configure SSL server certificates click Create new certificate and save the setting From Google Cloud CLI To enforce SSL encryption for an instance run the command: gcloud sql instances patch INSTANCE_NAME --ssl-mode= ENCRYPTED_ONLY Note: RESTART is required for type MySQL Generation 1 Instances (backendType: FIRST_GEN) to get this configuration in effect. Default Value: By default parameter settings: ipConfiguration: sslMode is not set which is equivalent to sslMode:ALLOW_UNENCRYPTED_AND_ENCRYPTED. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/configure-ssl-instance/ Additional Information: By default Settings: ipConfiguration has no authorizedNetworks set/configured. In that case even if by default sslMode is not set, which is equivalent to sslMode:ALLOW_UNENCRYPTED_AND_ENCRYPTED there is no risk as instance cannot be accessed outside of the network unless authorizedNetworks are configured. However, If default for sslMode is not updated to ENCRYPTED_ONLY any authorizedNetworks created later on will not enforce SSL only connection.
input_row.impact: After enforcing SSL requirement for connections, existing client will not be able to communicate with Cloud SQL database instance unless they use SSL encrypted connections to communicate to Cloud SQL database instance.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/configure-ssl-instance/ Additional Information: By default Settings: ipConfiguration has no authorizedNetworks set/configured. In that case even if by default sslMode is not set, which is equivalent to sslMode:ALLOW_UNENCRYPTED_AND_ENCRYPTED there is no risk as instance cannot be accessed outside of the network unless authorizedNetworks are configured. However, If default for sslMode is not updated to ENCRYPTED_ONLY any authorizedNetworks created later on will not enforce SSL only connection.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- ✓ Can GCP APIs query this property? → Yes
- ✓ Is this a standard GCP service property (encryption, logging, access control, network rules)? → Yes
- ✓ Does Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK have methods to check this? → Yes
- ✓ Does GCP CLI have commands to retrieve this? → Yes
- Does it require SSH/RDP to instances? → No
- ✓ Does audit mention Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI commands? → Yes

The control requires checking whether a Cloud SQL instance is configured to enforce SSL for all incoming connections, which is a standard GCP service property related to encryption and network rules. The audit instructions clearly provide a method to automate this check using the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud sql instances list`).

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_ssl_enforcement_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **GCP API/SDK Calls:** Use the Google Cloud SDK to run the command `gcloud sql instances list --format=json`.
- **Configuration Check:** Retrieve the `sslMode` property from the `ipConfiguration` section of each Cloud SQL instance.
- **Expected Setting:** The `sslMode` should be set to `ENCRYPTED_ONLY`.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** If `sslMode` is set to `ENCRYPTED_ONLY`, the instance is compliant.
  - **Fail:** If `sslMode` is not set to `ENCRYPTED_ONLY` or defaults to `ALLOW_UNENCRYPTED_AND_ENCRYPTED`, the instance is non-compliant.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** All Cloud SQL instances in the GCP project.
  
This approach allows for an automated and scalable method to ensure all Cloud SQL instances require SSL for incoming connections, leveraging GCP's robust API capabilities.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **PARTIALLY_AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. This control can and should be checked via automation, leveraging GCP APIs and the `gcloud` CLI.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Capability:**  
  **Discrepancy:** The assessment claims that the `sslMode` property in the `ipConfiguration` section can be checked via `gcloud sql instances list --format=json`. However, **as of current GCP documentation and API responses, there is no `sslMode` property directly available in the Cloud SQL instance resource**.  
  The enforcement of SSL connections is controlled via the `require_ssl` property on each authorized user, not at the instance level.  
  - The correct check is to enumerate all users of each Cloud SQL instance and verify that `require_ssl` is set to `true` for all users.
  - The `gcloud sql users list --instance=<INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json` command returns a `require_ssl` property per user.
- **CloudFormation/Config:**  
  GCP Config (now called Config Connector) does not natively check this property at the instance level; it must be checked per user.

**Conclusion:** The approach is automatable, but the property and method described are technically inaccurate.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 4/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  - The `gcloud sql instances list --format=json` command does not return an `sslMode` property.
  - The correct method is to use `gcloud sql users list` and check the `require_ssl` field for each user.
- **gcloud Methods/CLI Commands:**  
  - The command provided is not sufficient for this control.
  - The correct command is:  
    ```sh
    gcloud sql users list --instance=<INSTANCE_NAME> --format=json
    ```
    Then, for each user, verify `"requireSsl": true`.
- **Manual Steps:**  
  - Not applicable, as the control is automatable.
- **Program Name Convention:**  
  - The program name `gcp_sql_instance_ssl_enforcement_enabled` is **not sufficiently descriptive**. It should specify that the check is for user-level SSL enforcement, e.g., `gcp_sql_user_require_ssl_enabled`.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Correct the Technical Method:**  
  - Update the automation to enumerate all Cloud SQL instances, then for each instance, enumerate all users and check that `require_ssl` is `true`.
- **Alternative GCP API Methods:**  
  - Use the Cloud SQL Admin API:  
    - `instances.list` to enumerate instances.
    - `users.list` to enumerate users and check the `requireSsl` field.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - `gcp_sql_user_require_ssl_enabled`
  - Or, for clarity: `gcp_sql_instance_all_users_require_ssl`
- **Missing Considerations:**  
  - The assessment does not mention that **new users must also be created with `require_ssl=true`** and that this should be monitored continuously.
  - The assessment does not address **service accounts** or **IAM authentication**, which may bypass user-level SSL enforcement (should be noted as a limitation or additional check).

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **MEDIUM**

- The control is automatable, but the technical implementation described is incorrect.  
- The reviewer is confident in the correct approach, but the original assessment is technically flawed.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Modify** the assessment to:
  - Use the correct method: enumerate all users for each Cloud SQL instance and check `require_ssl=true`.
  - Update the program name to reflect user-level SSL enforcement.
  - Remove reference to the non-existent `sslMode` property.
  - Add a note about continuous monitoring for new users and potential bypasses via IAM authentication.

---

**Summary Table**

| Section              | Assessment         |
|----------------------|-------------------|
| Approach Validation  | PARTIALLY_AGREE   |
| Technical Accuracy   | 4/10              |
| Improvements         | See above         |
| Confidence           | MEDIUM            |
| Final Recommendation | Modify            |

---

**References:**
- [Cloud SQL User Management](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/create-manage-users)
- [gcloud sql users list](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/sql/users/list)
- [Cloud SQL Admin API: users.list](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/admin-api/rest/v1beta4/users/list)
